A.error_quality: structured-but-opaque - diagnostics revealed much (comments, visibility, ADT/contract syntax) but the parameter-binder token is masked as the internal symbol `__ANON_0`, so the one thing needed (how to name a parameter) is hidden.
A.notes: Pattern A STRICT (spec + --help + raw errors on own code only; no guide/SKILL.md/examples). Full 5-fix budget used (attempt_1..attempt_6). Reverse-engineered a lot from diagnostics: '--' comments; top-level needs public/private + data|fn; ADTs are 'public data Name { Variant(Type) }'; signature 'public fn name(@ParamType -> @ReturnType) requires() ensures() effects(pure) { <expr> }' (body is a trailing expression, no `return`). HARD WALL: after '@Type' the parser only ever says 'Expected one of: COMMA, __ANON_0' and rejects every concrete candidate for the parameter-binder (identifiers, all punctuation/operators, keywords) -- the needed token is the anonymous lexer symbol __ANON_0, only revealable via the (forbidden) guide. With no way to name the input parameter, the body cannot reference the JSON string, so a real recursive-descent parser is impossible. attempt_6 compiles AND runs but can only return a constant Err stub -> 13 valid cases fail (wrong value) and the 9 error cases pass only degenerately, so functional pass_count is 0/22 and pass_basis is null. Same blocker as observed for Vera under Pattern A in problem2.

attempts.failed_cases: all (parse error: E005 at ')' after @String -> COMMA/__ANON_0)
all (parse error: '@s' -> '@' must precede a TYPE)
compiles & runs but constant Err stub (no param name); 13 valid fail, 9 error degenerate -> functional 0/22

raw_error_excerpts: [E005] Unexpected ')' Expected one of: AT, __ANON_0 (empty '()' rejected)
[E005] Unexpected ')' Expected one of: COMMA, __ANON_0 (cannot bind a parameter name after '@String'; token masked as __ANON_0)
[E005] Unexpected 's' Expected one of: FN, LBRACE, UPPER_IDENT ('@' must precede a TYPE)
[E001] Function is missing its contract block (requires()/ensures()/effects() required)
[E005] body: Unexpected 'Err' after 'return'; Expected DOT, LPAR, __ANON_9 (no `return`; operators masked as __ANON_9)
A.toolchain_notes: vera 0.0.160 (Z3 bundled), /opt/venv/bin/vera. Used: vera --help, vera check[ --json], vera run --fn .. -- args. NO guide/SKILL.md/examples/web (Pattern A STRICT). Only attempt_1..6 + condition_result.json kept. Re-run under the codified strict 5-fix rule (replaces the earlier run that stopped at attempt_1).
A.reclassified_note: SKIPPED -> PARTIAL(0/22): the `vera` compiler runs fine; the failure is code-generation under Pattern A, not absence of toolchain.

reverify.command: vera check attempt_6.vera && vera run attempt_6.vera --fn parse -- <input>
reverify.result: compiles/runs but constant Err stub -> functional 0/22

B.error_quality: structured - natural-language diagnostics with codes (E130 unresolved slot with valid-index range, E005/E006 parse errors), explicit fix hints, and a clear 'unsupported effect Diverge - skipped' message pointing at the codegen limit.
B.notes: Pattern B (guide: bundled /opt/vera/SKILL.md). Hand-written recursive descent. ADT JVal { JNull,JBool,JInt,JFloat,JStr,JArr,JObj }; index-threaded parsers return Result<(JVal,Nat),String>. Comparison is by canonical re-serialization (ser) + string equality with expected canonical JSON -> pass_basis=normalized. attempt_1 modeled recursion with effects(<Diverge>) (unsupported by codegen) and had De-Bruijn slot errors; attempt_2 switched recursive fns to effects(pure) with decreases() and fixed slot-order/serialization bugs. No stdlib JSON.

failed_cases: could not run: effects(<Diverge>) unsupported by codegen; multiple E130 slot-index errors

raw_error_excerpts: [E130] Cannot resolve @Array<JVal>.1: only 1 Array<JVal> binding(s) in scope (valid indices: 0..0)
[E005] string literal inside \(...) interpolation not allowed
B.toolchain_notes: vera 0.0.160 (Z3 bundled). Guide source that worked: cat /opt/vera/SKILL.md (bundled). vera check / vera run. Key learning: no Diverge codegen; recursive fns need decreases() + effects(pure).
